# TESS Program G08103

Title: Measuring Planet Demographics Around Young M Stars

PI: Gillis, Erik - Mcmaster University

Type: SMALL

Summary: Planetary radii and periods are expected to evolve significantly over the first billion years of their lives, and these effects are imprinted on the field-age planet population. To characterize the drivers of this evolution, we must survey young stars. Recent results from the Gaia mission have uncovered thousands of young clusters hosting thousands of young M stars (<1 Gyr). Young field M stars are difficult to age, but cluster membership provides strong age constraints on the planets they host. We therefore propose to use TESS s unique capabilities and observing strategy to target a sample of 4135 young M stars in nearby clusters with expected sensitivity to planets of interest to measure their occurrence rate and uncover the dominant drivers of planetary evolution.


# Sector 097 targets observed (2 min: 277, 20 s: 6)
